headlines.title: Anthropic expands Mythos to 150 additional organizations in more than 15 countries
headlines.summary: Anthropic initially rolled out Project Glasswing to about 50 partners in April to test the model for cybersecurity flaws.

headlines.title: Trump picks housing Dir. Bill Pulte as acting intelligence chief, replacing Tulsi Gabbard
headlines.summary: Pulte will keep his existing titles as FHFA director and chairman of the mortgage groups Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, Trump said in his announcement.

headlines.title: Nvidia's new PC chips represent CEO Huang's bid to win at every layer of AI stack
headlines.summary: Nvidia's announced entry into the PC chip market sent shares of AMD, Intel and Qualcomm lower as Wall Street recognized the threat.

headlines.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80 billion from stock sales to fund AI build-out
headlines.summary: Alphabet said it plans to sell $80 billion in stock, including through a $10 billion investment by Berkshire Hathaway.

headlines.title: Marvell stock soars 20% as Nvidia's Huang says it could be the next trillion-dollar company
headlines.summary: Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, hailed this chip designer as the next trillion-dollar firm, sending its shares up sharply in early market trading.

headlines.title: Victoria Secret shares spike 40% after big earnings beat, raised sales outlook
headlines.summary: Victoria's Secret has been in the midst of a turnaround under CEO Hillary Super, who said the company is winning over young shoppers across all incomes.

headlines.title: Software stocks just passed a big milestone
headlines.summary: Software stocks are making a comeback from their artificial intelligence-driven sell-off earlier this year.

headlines.title: Berkshire Hathaway invests extra $10 billion in Alphabet, deepening bet on AI
headlines.summary: The deal builds on a stake Berkshire has steadily expanded since late 2025, making Alphabet one of its biggest recent bets.

headlines.title: Greg Abel just made his first big deal as Berkshire CEO. Why Warren Buffett is happy
headlines.summary: Berkshire's purchase of homebuilder Taylor Morrison gives the conglomerate a larger foothold in housing and appears to have been struck at a bargain valuation.

headlines.title: Ralph Lauren shares have flatlined in 2026. Here's how to profit anyway
headlines.summary: When investors sell strangles, they are selling an out of the money put and an out of the money call.

headlines.title: Top Wall Street analysts see robust growth potential in these 3 stocks
headlines.summary: Investors keen on capitalizing on the AI boom can track recommendations from top Wall Street analysts

headlines.title: Berkshire trails red-hot S&P 500 by biggest margin so far this year
headlines.summary: Berkshire's widely held B shares are now running 16.3 percentage points behind the benchmark index year-to-date, the biggest gap so far in 2026.

headlines.title: Why the most important company enabling AI isn’t Nvidia, according to this fund manager
headlines.summary: Portfolio manager Jonathan Cofsky talks about where to find the winners in the AI landscape and one company that is beating all comers.

headlines.title: ‘I have no preexisting conditions’: I’m 56, earn $198,000 and want to retire early. Can I afford private healthcare?
headlines.summary: “I’m trying to figure out whether it’s worth my while achieving FIRE.”

headlines.title: Silicon Valley was built on public money — now it’s fighting California’s billionaire tax
headlines.summary: “Cyberselfish” author says tech elite believe that taxes are for “little people.”

headlines.title: Many big oil tankers remain stuck in the Strait of Hormuz — and may not return once they escape
headlines.summary: There’s doubt massive tankers, once the backbone of global crude flows, will be returning in quite the same numbers to the Middle East.

headlines.title: It’s official: More money is now spent building data centers than the government spends on transportation
headlines.summary: A notable milestone was quietly reached in the April construction-spending report released by the Census Bureau on Monday: Construction spending on data centers overtook public spending on transportation.

headlines.title: Many Americans now need $20,000 in an emergency savings fund. Yes, really.
headlines.summary: The old rules of thumb are broken — here is why a three-month cash buffer is ‘almost dangerous’

observations: High concentration in Communication Services sector, primarily driven by META.
Cash position at 11.66% exceeds the minimum cash buffer requirement of 5%, but may need to be deployed to reduce sector concentration.
suggested_changes: Trim META by ~7 share(s) to bring weight under 25%
Consider reallocating excess cash into sectors aligned with investor preferences, such as AI / compute infrastructure or clean energy.
open_questions: What is the investor's current view on increasing exposure to preferred sectors given the high cash position?
Is there a specific target for cash deployment to achieve a more balanced sector allocation?

candidates.thesis: Qualcomm is a leader in semiconductor technology, particularly in 5G and AI-enabled devices. The company is well-positioned to benefit from the secular growth in AI and compute infrastructure, with strong fundamentals and a reasonable valuation. RSI is below 70, and the stock is trading near its 52-week high, indicating solid momentum without being overbought.

Cameco is a leading uranium producer, benefiting from the global push toward clean energy and the growing interest in small modular reactors. The stock is trading near its 50-day SMA, with RSI below 70, and has strong secular tailwinds in the clean energy sector.

Onto Innovation specializes in advanced metrology and inspection solutions for semiconductor manufacturing. The company is poised to benefit from secular growth in AI and compute infrastructure. The stock is trading near its 50-day SMA, with RSI in a healthy range, and offers a strong risk-reward setup.

Quanta Services is a leader in infrastructure solutions for renewable energy and electric grid modernization. The company is well-positioned to benefit from the global energy transition. The stock has pulled back from its 52-week high and is trading near its 50-day SMA, with RSI below 50, indicating a potential entry point.

NVIDIA is a dominant player in AI and GPU technology, benefiting from the secular growth in AI and compute infrastructure. While the stock is trading near its 52-week high, its RSI is below 70, and the long-term growth potential justifies a high-conviction entry.
